content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

July 9, 1992

Michael Franchetti  
Franchetti & Franchetti  
1121 L Street, Suite 610  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Follow-up to Our Advice Letter, File No. I-91-301

Dear Mr. Franchetti:

This is in regard to our advice letter to you which concerned the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 (Advice Letter No. I-91-301, dated August 15, 1991.)

On April 1, 1992, Allan Tebbetts, on behalf of the Institute of Governmental Advocates, requested the Fair Political Practices Commission to reconsider the advice contained in that letter. Specifically, Mr. Tebbetts asked reconsideration of the following advice:

Participation by the corporation employee in a discussion with an official concerning administrative action would be considered "direct communication" subject to the tests in Regulation 18239. This is true whether or not the employee is accompanied by a lobbyist. [Footnote 4: This advice differs from previous advice contained in an information sheet (copy enclosed) that describes common situations in which the contacts test should be applied. This information sheet is being reviewed and will be revised in the near future.]

The Commission considered this advice on July 2, 1992, and decided that the advice in the letter should be brought into

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

compliance with past Commission advice until the Commission has the opportunity to take public testimony on the issue.

Consequently, the current rule is:

A company officer or employee meets with a legislator (or the legislator's aide):

(a) along with the company's paid, registered lobbyist -- one contact for the lobbyist; no contact for the officer

We will notify you if the Commission determines to reinstate the advice in Advice Letter No. I-91-301, or to otherwise modify the existing advice.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.1

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

1 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10¢ per page.
